napa-cabbage-wedge-with-sesame-ranch.name: Napa Cabbage Wedge With Sesame Ranch

ingredients.prep: mild red pepper flakes (such as Urfa biber or Aleppo-style; optional), and toasted sesame seeds (for serving)

instructions: Cook bacon in a single layer in a large skillet over medium heat, turning occasionally, until browned and crisp, 10–12 minutes. Transfer to paper towels and let cool.
Meanwhile, purée olive oil, vinegar, miso, tahini, onion powder, soy sauce, sesame oil, and 2 Tbsp. water in a blender until smooth; season with salt and pepper.

Trim top and bottom from cabbage and slice center portion into 1½"-thick rounds. Spoon 1–2 Tbsp. dressing onto each plate and top each with a cabbage round. Season cabbage lightly with salt. Drizzle 1–2 Tbsp. dressing over each and work it into the layers with the spoon. Drizzle any remaining dressing over.
Tear bacon into large pieces and scatter over cabbage. Top with chives, mild red pepper flakes, and sesame seeds as desired.

nectarine-kebabs-with-chocolate-and-pist.name: Nectarine Kebabs With Chocolate And Pistachio
nectarine-kebabs-with-chocolate-and-pist.original_name: Espetadas de nectarinas com chocolate e pistácio
instructions: Break the chocolate in pieces and melt them in a sea bath. Add the milk and butter and stir until it melts and the mixture is homogeneous.
Peel the clementine and separate the buds; cut the pineapple into pieces; cut the melon into balls, with the help of a Parisian spoon; fix the strawberries and cut the nectarines into pieces.
Place the fruit, interspersed, on four wooden spikes, starting with nectarine and ending with strawberry.

Gently crush the pistachios. Pass the end of the strawberries through the chocolate sauce and then through the pistachios. Water the fruit with yarn from the chocolate sauce and put the rest in a saucer.
Slightly sprinkle the spikes and sauce with the remaining pistachios and serve cold or at room temperature.

no-bake-chocolate-oatmeal-cookies.name: No-bake Chocolate Oatmeal Cookies
instructions: Mix sugar, milk, butter, and cocoa powder in a large saucepan and bring to a rolling boil. Boil, stirring frequently, for about 1 minute. Remove from heat and add oats, coconut, peanut butter, and vanilla. Mix thoroughly.
Drop tablespoon-sized balls of cookie dough onto waxed paper and let cool in refrigerator for at least 1 hour.

northern-italian-style-chicken-cacciator.name: Northern Italian-style Chicken Cacciatore

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Rinse chicken pieces and pat dry.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and brown, 4 to 5 minutes per side. Remove chicken to a plate.
Add mushrooms, onion, and celery to the pan drippings and sauté until soft, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 2 more minutes.

Return chicken to the skillet. Add white wine and cook until it has almost completely evaporated, 2 to 3 minutes. Add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, parsley, oregano, pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Cover and simmer until chicken is no longer pink at the bone and the juices run clear, about 30 minutes.

ny-cheesecake-cupcakes-argiro.name: Ny Cheesecake Cupcakes
instructions: Beat the cold cream first until it stands. Keep it in the refrigerator.
All ingredients for the cream must be at room temperature.
We'll need a muffin form with 12 cases. Place Cupcake cards inside. Place from a stuffed biscuit on each paper as a base.
Preheat oven to 170oC.
In bowls add the cheese and sugar and hand mixer at low speed, work to melt the sugar and fluffy the mixture.
Add the eggs to the mixture. Add the vanilla.
We balance the cheese cream in the cases.
Bake for 18′ to stabilize. Let them cool well. Refrigerate for 2 hours.
Melt the chocolate and dip each strawberry in half.
Touch one in each cupcake and garnish with whipped cream and mini biscuits.
